name: Mixed Public + Admin Shell
discovery_hints: Prefer route registry files under src/routes when present.
Use existing integration markers before editing src/App.tsx.
Keep admin pages behind the existing protected admin layout.
mount_expectations: Public surface modules are reachable from a public route.
Private surface modules are registered in admin routes and admin navigation.
Style utilities used by installed modules exist in Tailwind config or CSS layers.

agent_brief.summary: Mixed-capable shell with login, auth providers, and a protected admin area already wired. It starts with a neutral root entry, not a public landing. Public modules own public website/content only when the user explicitly needs a public surface; private modules own admin surface.
do_not: Do not treat src/pages/PublicEntry.tsx as a marketing landing.
Do not add public website pages unless the user asks for a public website, public booking/order flow, catalogue, or similar public surface.
Do not put admin-only screens on public routes.
Do not make write/admin endpoints public.
Do not rewrite src/App.tsx to add module pages; add routes in src/routes/publicRoutes.tsx or src/routes/adminRoutes.tsx.
Do not create a second admin layout; reuse src/components/layout/AdminLayout.tsx and src/config/navigation.ts.
Do not mention shell/template internals to the end user.
